method_atom: Analysis of the relaxation/convergence rate of a local-update, single-site Markov chain toward its equilibrium distribution over configurations on a hierarchical, tree-structured domain, via lower bounds on the spectral gap and the log-Sobolev constant. The central result is that fixed conditions imposed on the leaves/boundary of the tree can force fast (logarithmic-in-size) convergence uniformly across all values of the control parameters, even in a regime with multiple coexisting equilibria, in contrast to slow convergence under unconstrained boundaries. The same functional-inequality machinery extends to processes with hard exclusion constraints on allowed configurations.
object_atom: Statistical-mechanical models on n-vertex regular trees updated by Glauber dynamics: the Ising model with plus-boundary conditions across all temperatures and external fields, and hard-constraint models including the zero-temperature antiferromagnetic Potts model (proper colorings) and the hard-core lattice gas (independent sets).
brief_reason: The Markov-chain mixing-time / spectral-gap / log-Sobolev functional-inequality analysis on tree-structured state spaces is the transferable method, while the tree-based Ising and hard-constraint lattice-gas/coloring spin systems are the domain-specific objects.
